summary: Matched two functions across fighter and stage code: ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA reached 100% and grGreens_80214B58 reached 100%. The PR also notes unresolved data/string ownership around CaptureWaitKirby and thrownkirby; reviewer feedback says shared strings imply the files/units have to be merged.

key_files.role: Matched ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA and adjusted helper calls, stick-direction argument handling, scale source, includes, and local matching shape.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c added 16 deleted 24 hunks 5
raw_diff: ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c hunk changing includes, adding ftCo_800BD6EC_noinline, changing inlineB0, and changing ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report lists ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA +197 bytes, 63.61% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Matched grGreens_80214B58 by reshaping Green Greens bomb table initialization and BOMB helper expression.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/gr/grgreens.c added 12 deleted 7 hunks 1
raw_diff: grgreens.c hunk changing BOMB and grGreens_80214B58 initialization
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report lists grGreens_80214B58 +9 bytes, 99.26% to 100.00%

what_changed: Removed unused placeholder/platform/type/baselib/dolphin includes and removed local float/double constants from ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c.
Added ftCo_800BD6EC_noinline as a wrapper that calls ftCo_800BD6EC, and ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA now calls the wrapper before inlineB0 inside the ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A38 branch.
Changed ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A38 argument from fp to gobj->user_data in ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA.

Changed inlineB0 to compute a signed integer direction value of -1 or 1 from lstick.x and pass that to ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A04 instead of passing the absolute stick magnitude only in the negative branch.
Changed HSD_JObjSetScale in ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA to use fp->mv.co.capturekirby.scale rather than jobj->scale.
Changed grgreens BOMB helper from temporaries c/r to a nested ternary assignment using grGr_params->x20 and HSD_Randi.
Changed grGreens_80214B58 initialization from a loop over 0x20 entries to a loop over 24 entries plus explicit zero writes for indices 0x18 through 0x1D before BOMB calls.

smart_moves.title: Used bot match report as narrow verification evidence
smart_moves.text: The PR includes an automated report showing exactly two new matches and their byte gains, grounding the matching claim for ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA and grGreens_80214B58.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report for GALE01 lists 2 new matches

smart_moves.title: Kept data/string ownership concern explicit instead of forcing linkage
smart_moves.text: The PR body says CaptureWaitKirby could be linked if data was figured out, but shared strings with thrownkirby were unresolved. This preserves a boundary between text matching and data/TU ownership work.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR body says CaptureWaitKirby could be linked if data was figured out but shares strings with thrownkirby

decomp_lessons.lesson: Shared string ownership can block or complicate linking a decompiled fighter file even when function text matching improves.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR body notes CaptureWaitKirby could be linked if data was figured out but shares strings with thrownkirby
human_pr_text: ribbanya comment says if they share strings then they have to be merged

decomp_lessons.lesson: For table initialization matching, a natural loop may cover only the contiguous prefix while later slots are emitted as explicit stores; do not assume a full-array loop is always the authored/matching shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grGreens_80214B58 loop changed from i < 0x20 to i < 24 plus explicit zero stores for 0x18..0x1D
text_corpus: bot report shows grGreens_80214B58 reached 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a helper expects direction, matching may require passing a sign value derived from stick input rather than the absolute stick magnitude.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inlineB0 now sets value to -1 or 1 based on fp->input.lstick.x and passes value to ftKb_SpecialN_800F5A04
text_corpus: bot report shows ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA reached 100.00%

naming_conventions.name: BOMB
naming_conventions.lesson: The PR retains the existing uppercase local helper name BOMB for Green Greens bomb-slot initialization; evidence supports this as local file convention but not a global naming rule.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static inline void BOMB(int idx) retained in grgreens.c

naming_conventions.name: ftCo_800BD6EC_noinline
naming_conventions.lesson: The newly added wrapper keeps the address-style base symbol and appends _noinline, describing the matching tactic without inventing semantic meaning.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: declaration and definition of ftCo_800BD6EC_noinline calling ftCo_800BD6EC

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Noinline wrapper to alter call/inlining shape
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: ftCo_800BD6EC_noinline was added as a separate wrapper around ftCo_800BD6EC and ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA calls the wrapper. The bot report supports the resulting function match, but the PR does not explicitly explain the exact codegen reason.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800BD6EC_noinline added and called from ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA
text_corpus: bot report shows ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA reached 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: A local s32 pad was added at the start of ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA. This appears to be a matching tactic, but the PR slice does not include explicit objdiff details beyond the bot match report.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA adds s32 pad
text_corpus: bot report shows ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby_IASA reached 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Inline temporary removal and nested ternary shaping
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: grgreens BOMB removed temporaries c and r and replaced them with a single nested ternary assignment, matching grGreens_80214B58 according to the bot report.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: BOMB helper rewritten from c/r temporaries to nested ternary assignment
text_corpus: bot report shows grGreens_80214B58 reached 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Partial loop plus explicit stores for array initialization
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: grGreens_80214B58 uses a loop for indices 0..23 and explicit stores for 0x18..0x1D rather than one loop through 0x20. This is supported as matching for this function but should not be generalized without local evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grGreens_80214B58 loop and explicit stores
text_corpus: bot report shows grGreens_80214B58 reached 100.00%

The string-sharing/TU-merge point is supported by PR body and reviewer comment, but the slice does not include the actual shared strings or the resulting merge implementation.
No independent build, objdiff logs, or source files beyond the diff were loaded; validation claims are limited to the bot report in the PR evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Shared strings can require TU merge work before a file can be linked
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2559, the author stated CaptureWaitKirby could be linked if data was figured out but it shared strings with thrownkirby; reviewer ribbanya replied that if they share strings then they have to be merged. Curator should treat this as PR-local evidence for data/TU ownership constraints, not as proof of the exact shared-string set.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR body
human_pr_text: ribbanya issue comment

accepted_candidate_records.title: Partial loop plus explicit stores matched grGreens_80214B58
accepted_candidate_records.text: The grGreens_80214B58 match changed a full 0x20 loop into a loop over 24 entries followed by explicit zero stores for 0x18 through 0x1D, then bomb-slot setup. This is useful as a search precedent for array initialization shape mismatches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grgreens.c hunk
text_corpus: bot report grGreens_80214B58 100.00%

source_update_candidates.title: Shared string ownership may imply TU merge rather than isolated linking
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding or cross-linking a standard note under data-sections-and-tu-splits: when two candidate TUs share strings, string ownership can be evidence that the files/units need to be merged before linking, rather than forcing separate C files with fake data ownership.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author says CaptureWaitKirby shares strings with thrownkirby
human_pr_text: reviewer says if they share strings then they have to be merged
source_update_candidates.reason: Reviewer feedback directly states the merge implication, but the slice lacks the actual strings and broader precedent; curator should decide whether this belongs in global standards.

source_update_candidates.title: CaptureWaitKirby and thrownkirby shared-string linkage concern
source_update_candidates.text: For src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c, PR #2559 records that linking was blocked/complicated because data was not figured out and strings were shared with thrownkirby.
evidence_refs: raw_pr_json: PR body
human_pr_text: PR body and reviewer comment
source_update_candidates.reason: This is potentially useful path-specific historical context, but should not be promoted without curator review because the actual shared string symbols are not in the slice.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize s32 pad or noinline wrapper use as accepted style; the PR only provides a bot match result, not a detailed justification.
Do not claim CaptureWaitKirby was linked in this PR; the author explicitly says it could be linked if data was figured out, and the slice only shows function matching.
Do not infer the exact thrownkirby source path or shared string names from this slice; they are not shown.
Do not promote the grgreens partial-loop shape into a global rule; it is a local matching precedent for grGreens_80214B58.
